name: Sudan-Chad Displacement Corridor 2023-2024
description: A humanitarian corridor notebook focused on displacement from Sudan into eastern Chad, the border points handling arrivals, and the camp system that absorbed them.

sides.name: Displaced civilians
sides.description: The movement is driven by civilians fleeing fighting, repeated attacks, and the collapse of safety in parts of Darfur.

subUnits.name: Displaced households fleeing Darfur
subUnits.description:
Families and individuals fleeing violence in Darfur moved toward the nearest available crossing points into Chad.
- Role: Core population in motion
- Geographic anchor: Flows from Darfur, especially around El Geneina, toward Adré and nearby crossings in eastern Chad.
- Why it matters: The whole map exists to show where people are coming from, where they first reach safety, and where bottlenecks form.
- What to watch: Arrival volume, family separation, shelter needs, and whether onward relocation is available.

sides.name: Reception and aid response
sides.description: Chadian authorities and international responders turned a border shock into a camp, transport, and food-security problem.

subUnits.name: Chadian reception authorities
subUnits.description:
Border and local authorities absorbed first arrivals and managed the immediate transition from crossing point to organized reception or relocation.
- Role: Border management and first reception
- Geographic anchor: Centered on Adré and other eastern Chadian entry points facing Darfur.
- Why it matters: These authorities determine whether the border strip becomes a humanitarian dead end or a functioning entry corridor.
- What to watch: Initial registration, crowding, transport availability, and security near the border.

subUnits.name: UNHCR and WFP response system
subUnits.description:
International humanitarian agencies handled camp support, relocation, shelter, food, and the coordination picture for the emergency.
- Role: Camp sustainment and relief coordination
- Geographic anchor: Spread across Adré, Farchana, and deeper camp locations in eastern Chad.
- Why it matters: They convert arrival numbers into practical questions of land, logistics, food distribution, and protection.
- What to watch: Camp saturation, funding gaps, rainy season access, and the shift from emergency shelter to longer-term support.

sides.name: Conflict driver
sides.description: The fighting in Sudan is the source pressure behind the displacement corridor even when the notebook's main operational view is humanitarian.

subUnits.name: Darfur conflict zone
subUnits.description:
Violence in and around Darfur, especially West Darfur, repeatedly drove new departures toward Chad.
- Role: Source pressure behind displacement
- Geographic anchor: Concentrated around El Geneina and surrounding areas where attacks triggered flight toward the frontier.
- Why it matters: Without the source geography, the border emergency becomes detached from the conflict that creates it.
- What to watch: Changes in violence intensity and whether they produce fresh displacement waves.

events.title: War breaks out in Sudan
events.description: Nationwide conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces begins, creating the conditions for large-scale displacement from Darfur into Chad.

events.title: Violence in West Darfur drives mass flight to Adré
events.description: Attacks in and around El Geneina sent large numbers of people toward Adré, rapidly turning the border area into an emergency reception site.

events.title: Road and camp access deteriorates in the rains
events.description: Seasonal weather compounded the crisis by making roads harder to use and stretching food, water, and shelter support across difficult terrain.

events.title: Relocation deeper into Chad becomes a priority
events.description: Humanitarian planning shifted toward moving people farther from the border into camps that offered somewhat more room and security than the initial reception strip.

events.title: Arrivals continue and camp saturation deepens
events.description: The crisis persisted into 2024, with new arrivals continuing to stress already crowded camps and service networks in eastern Chad.

events.title: The corridor hardens into a protracted emergency
events.description: What began as a border shock increasingly resembled a protracted displacement crisis with long-term needs around shelter, food, and protection.

meta.name: El Geneina
meta.description: West Darfur urban area repeatedly associated with intense violence and resulting displacement toward Chad.

meta.name: Adré border crossing and reception area
meta.description: Border town in Chad that became one of the main reception points for people fleeing Darfur.

meta.name: Farchana camp system
meta.description: Camp and services zone in eastern Chad representing the deeper humanitarian system beyond the immediate frontier.

meta.name: Darfur to Chad displacement corridor
meta.description: Simplified movement line from violence-affected areas in Darfur to border reception and deeper camp systems inside Chad.

meta.name: Eastern Chad camp basin
meta.description: Illustrative basin showing the wider concentration of camps and services across eastern Chad.
